summary: Implement an Azure Function that nightly queries GPT-4o via TRAPI for the 1985 New York Yankees roster and stores the results in Azure Blob Storage, using Managed Identity for all authentication (no API keys).
assumptions: TRAPI refers to the internal GitHub/Microsoft TRAPI gateway for accessing GPT-4o; a TRAPI endpoint and Managed Identity-based auth flow are available to the team.
The Azure Function will use a Timer Trigger set to a nightly schedule (e.g., 0 0 2 * * *).
Python is the preferred language given the simplicity of the workload, unless the team specifies otherwise.
Azure Consumption (Serverless) plan is sufficient given the low-frequency nightly execution.
A single Azure Storage Account and Blob container will store the roster output as a JSON or CSV file.
GitHub Actions will be used for CI/CD.
Bicep will be used for IaC.
No VNet integration is required unless the TRAPI endpoint is network-restricted.
The plan is NOT automatically approved and requires explicit sign-off.
open_questions: What is the exact TRAPI endpoint URL, authentication scope, and request schema for GPT-4o queries?
Should the blob output overwrite a single file nightly or append versioned files (e.g., timestamped)?
Which Azure subscription, resource group, and region should be targeted?
Is there an existing Azure Function App or storage account to reuse, or should all resources be net-new?
What language/runtime is preferred for the Azure Function (Python 3.11, C# .NET 8, etc.)?
Are there alerting channels (Teams, email, PagerDuty) for failure notifications?
Does the TRAPI endpoint require network allowlisting for the Function App's outbound IP?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Provision all Azure infrastructure and establish CI/CD pipeline so subsequent feature work can deploy into a stable environment.
entry_criteria: Azure subscription and resource group confirmed
TRAPI endpoint details and auth scope documented
GitHub repository available with branch protection enabled
exit_criteria: Azure Function App, Storage Account, and App Insights provisioned via Bicep
Managed Identity assigned with correct RBAC roles
CI/CD pipeline deploys a hello-world function successfully

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement the nightly Timer Trigger function that calls TRAPI/GPT-4o and writes roster data to Blob Storage.
entry_criteria: INF-01 and INF-02 complete — infrastructure exists
SEC-01 complete — Managed Identity has Blob and TRAPI permissions
TRAPI endpoint and request/response schema confirmed
exit_criteria: Function retrieves 1985 Yankees roster from GPT-4o via TRAPI without API keys
Roster is written to the designated Blob container
Timer Trigger fires on nightly schedule in dev environment

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Add reliability, error handling, observability, and security controls to production-harden the function.
entry_criteria: Core Build wave complete and function end-to-end verified in dev
exit_criteria: Retry policy and poison-run handling implemented
Structured logs and Application Insights alerts configured
No secrets in code or configuration; all access via Managed Identity
Key Vault used for any non-identity config values

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Validate end-to-end in a staging environment, complete rollout to production, and confirm operational readiness.
entry_criteria: Staging environment provisioned (or dev promoted to staging)
exit_criteria: End-to-end test passes in staging: nightly trigger fires, roster retrieved, blob written
Monitoring and alerting verified active in production
All success criteria from spec.md checked off

tasks.title: Review and approve implementation task plan
tasks.description: Owner (rciapala) reviews this task plan, resolves open questions (TRAPI endpoint, language choice, blob naming strategy), and provides explicit sign-off before implementation begins.
outputs: Approved task plan
Resolved open questions documented in repo
acceptance_criteria: All open questions answered or deferred with rationale
Task plan approved in writing (PR comment or issue)
Language/runtime for the Azure Function confirmed
TRAPI auth scope and endpoint URL documented
risks: Unanswered TRAPI integration questions can block APP-01 and APP-02
Unresolved language/runtime choice delays INF-01 configuration

tasks.title: Provision Azure infrastructure via Bicep IaC
tasks.description: Author Bicep templates to provision the Azure Function App (Consumption plan), Azure Storage Account with a dedicated Blob container for roster output, and Application Insights workspace. All resources should be tagged and deployed to the confirmed resource group.
inputs: Confirmed Azure subscription, resource group, region
outputs: Bicep templates in /infra directory
Deployed dev environment resources
Resource names documented
acceptance_criteria: Bicep templates are idempotent and deployable via `az deployment group create`
Azure Function App created with correct runtime stack (Python/C# per PM-01 decision)
Storage Account and Blob container created with private access (no anonymous read)
Application Insights connected to the Function App
All resources tagged with project name and owner
risks: Subscription quota limits may require requesting additional capacity
Region availability for Consumption plan should be confirmed upfront

tasks.title: Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline
tasks.description: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs tests, builds the function artifact, and deploys to Azure using OIDC federated identity (no stored credentials). Include environment gates for dev and production deployments.
inputs: INF-01 outputs (Function App name, resource group)
outputs: `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`
OIDC federated credential configured in Azure AD
acceptance_criteria: Pipeline deploys successfully on push to main branch
OIDC used for Azure authentication — no service principal secret stored in GitHub
Test step runs before deployment; deployment blocked on test failure
Manual approval gate present before production deployment
risks: OIDC setup requires Azure AD app registration permissions that may need admin approval

tasks.title: Configure Managed Identity and RBAC for Function App
tasks.description: Enable system-assigned Managed Identity on the Function App. Assign Storage Blob Data Contributor role for the roster Blob container, and configure the TRAPI-required identity scope/role so GPT-4o can be called without API keys.
outputs: Managed Identity assigned
RBAC role assignments documented and applied via Bicep
acceptance_criteria: System-assigned Managed Identity enabled on the Function App
Storage Blob Data Contributor role scoped to the roster container (not account-level)
TRAPI identity scope configured and verified by calling the endpoint from the Function App identity
No API keys, connection strings with secrets, or credentials present in app settings or code
risks: TRAPI may require a specific AAD role or group membership not self-serviceable by the team
Overly broad RBAC scope increases blast radius if the identity is compromised

tasks.title: Implement TRAPI/GPT-4o roster retrieval module
tasks.description: Write the function module that constructs a prompt requesting the 1985 Yankees roster, calls the GPT-4o model via TRAPI using Managed Identity token acquisition (azure-identity), and parses and validates the response into a structured roster list.
inputs: TRAPI endpoint URL and request schema (from PM-01)
outputs: Roster retrieval module with unit tests
Structured roster data (list of player dicts)
acceptance_criteria: Module acquires a Managed Identity token and authenticates to TRAPI without API keys
Prompt returns a parseable list of all 1985 Yankees players (roster completeness verifiable against known historical record)
Response parsed into a consistent schema (e.g., [{name, position, number}])
Unit tests cover happy path, TRAPI error response, and malformed response
No credentials hardcoded or logged
risks: GPT-4o may return incomplete or hallucinated roster data — consider cross-referencing against a known source
TRAPI rate limits or quota could cause intermittent failures

tasks.title: Implement Blob Storage write module
tasks.description: Write the module that serializes the roster data to JSON (or CSV) and uploads it to the designated Azure Blob container using the azure-storage-blob SDK authenticated via Managed Identity (DefaultAzureCredential).
inputs: INF-01 Storage Account and container details
outputs: Blob write module with unit tests
Roster stored as `1985-yankees-roster.json` (or timestamped variant per PM-01 decision)
acceptance_criteria: Blob is written using DefaultAzureCredential — no storage account key in config
File is readable and valid JSON after upload
Module is unit-tested with a mocked BlobServiceClient
Overwrite or versioning strategy matches PM-01 decision
risks: Managed Identity propagation delay after role assignment can cause 403 errors in early testing

tasks.title: Implement nightly Timer Trigger Azure Function
tasks.description: Wire APP-01 and APP-02 modules into an Azure Function with a Timer Trigger set to execute nightly (e.g., `0 0 2 * * *` UTC). Ensure the function is idempotent and logs execution result and player count.
outputs: Timer Trigger function entry point
function.json / decorator config with cron schedule
Deployed to dev Function App via INF-02 pipeline
acceptance_criteria: Timer Trigger cron expression verified to fire nightly
Function runs end-to-end: retrieves roster from TRAPI, writes to Blob
Function is idempotent — re-running does not corrupt or duplicate data
Player count and blob write confirmation logged on each execution
risks: Cold start on Consumption plan may add latency but is acceptable for nightly batch
Clock skew or timezone misconfiguration could shift execution time

tasks.title: Implement retry policy and failure handling
tasks.description: Add retry logic with exponential backoff for TRAPI calls and Blob write operations. Implement structured error handling so transient failures are retried and persistent failures are logged with actionable context before the function exits with a non-zero status.
outputs: Updated modules with retry decorators or SDK retry policies
Error handling unit tests
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI calls retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff on 429 and 5xx responses
Blob write retries on transient network errors
Final failure raises an exception that marks the function run as failed in Application Insights
Unit tests simulate transient and permanent failure scenarios
risks: Overly aggressive retry on TRAPI quota errors could exhaust daily token limits

tasks.title: Secrets and configuration hardening
tasks.description: Audit all application settings and configuration to confirm zero secrets are stored as plaintext. Route any non-identity config values (e.g., TRAPI endpoint URL, blob container name) through Azure App Configuration or Key Vault references in Function App settings.
outputs: Security checklist completed
Key Vault / App Config references in place if needed
CI lint/secret-scan step added to INF-02 pipeline
acceptance_criteria: GitHub secret scanning and push protection enabled on the repository
Zero secrets, connection strings with keys, or credentials in source code or Function App settings
TRAPI endpoint URL stored as a non-secret app setting (not hardcoded)
Key Vault reference used if any sensitive config value is needed beyond Managed Identity
risks: Key Vault reference setup requires additional RBAC (Key Vault Secrets User) on the Function App identity

tasks.title: Configure structured logging, metrics, and alerts
tasks.description: Ensure the function emits structured logs (JSON) to Application Insights including execution timestamp, player count retrieved, blob write status, and any errors. Create an alert rule that fires when the nightly function fails or does not execute within the expected window.
outputs: Structured logging implemented in function code
Application Insights alert rules for failure and missed execution
Alert notification channel configured
acceptance_criteria: Each function execution logs: run timestamp, player count, blob URL written, success/failure status
Application Insights alert fires within 5 minutes of a failed function execution
A 'missed execution' alert fires if no successful run is recorded within 26 hours
Logs are queryable via KQL in Application Insights
risks: Missed-execution alert requires careful threshold tuning to avoid false positives on minor schedule drift

tasks.title: End-to-end validation in staging environment
tasks.description: Deploy the complete solution to a staging environment (or promote dev) and perform an end-to-end validation: manually trigger the function, confirm roster is retrieved from TRAPI, verify the blob is written correctly, and confirm Application Insights logs and alerts are firing.
inputs: All Core Build and Hardening tasks complete
outputs: QA sign-off checklist
Evidence artifacts (screenshots, blob content, log excerpts)
acceptance_criteria: Manual trigger in staging produces a valid 1985 Yankees roster in the Blob container
Blob file is valid JSON and contains a complete roster (cross-referenced against historical record)
Application Insights shows structured log entries for the triggered run
Simulated failure (e.g., wrong container name) triggers the failure alert
No secrets found in code, settings, or logs during review
risks: Staging TRAPI quota may be separate from production and could be limited
Historical roster completeness validation requires a trusted reference source

tasks.title: Production deployment and go-live verification
tasks.description: Execute the production deployment via the approved CI/CD pipeline, verify the nightly Timer Trigger is scheduled correctly in the production Function App, confirm monitoring is active, and hand off operational runbook to the team.
outputs: Production Function App running with nightly schedule
Operational runbook documenting manual trigger, monitoring checks, and rollback steps
acceptance_criteria: Production deployment executed via CI/CD pipeline with manual approval gate passed
Next scheduled nightly execution confirmed in Function App monitor view
Application Insights and alerts confirmed active in production
Rollback procedure documented: redeploy previous artifact via pipeline
All spec.md success criteria checked off and signed by rciapala
risks: Production Managed Identity may need separate TRAPI role assignment from staging
First nightly run may not occur until the following day — schedule a manual validation trigger post-deploy
